ground_truth.title: What's the Cost of Social Advertising?
ground_truth.author: WebFX
ground_truth.main_content: With more than five billion active users around the world–approximately 63.9% of the global population–social media platforms are the ideal place to promote your products or services to your target audience. But how much does advertising cost for social media? Average social media advertising pricing can cost anywhere from $15 to $200 per day to $450 to $6,000 per month to manage an account (paid to your agency or consultant), with monthly ad spend ranging from $200 to over $50,000 (paid to ad networks). Social advertising, also known as social media advertising, is a digital marketing strategy in which companies run paid ad campaigns on the social media platform (or platforms) of their choice. According to a 2023 report on consumer behavior, approximately 58% of shoppers in the US said they bought a product after seeing it on social media. Implementing social media advertising in your digital marketing strategy can benefit your business in many ways, including building brand awareness, providing high-reward relatively low-cost marketing, and giving access to valuable analytics. If you need help maximizing your ad results or simply don't have enough time or resources to manage your campaign, you might opt to partner with an experienced social media advertising agency. Social media marketing prices range from $450 to $6,000 per month. When you partner with an award-winning social media advertising agency, you'll receive a dedicated account manager, custom ad strategy, unique ad design, compelling ad copy, advanced demographic and interest targeting, ongoing reports and campaign analysis, continuous campaign management and optimization. The Duolingo case study demonstrates how the most effective use of your social media ad budget is to amplify content that people would want to watch anyway. LinkedIn is generally the most expensive platform, with an average Cost-Per-Click (CPC) often exceeding $5.00. CPC means you pay every time someone clicks on your ad, while CPM means you pay for every 1,000 times your ad is shown. You can lower your social media ad costs by improving your ad quality and relevance score, narrowing your audience targeting, A/B testing your creative and copy, and focusing on ad formats with higher engagement. The most effective strategy uses both organic social media for building community and paid advertising for reaching new audiences at scale.
with: How much does social media advertising cost? Average social media advertising usually costs between $15 to $200 per day, and monthly ad spend ranges between $200 to over $50,000 per month.
The cost is affected by factors such as the platform used, the target audience, industry competition, and the ad quality.
Businesses typically pay based on models like cost-per-click (CPC) or cost-per-thousand-impressions (CPM).
Duolingo's primary investment isn't in slick, high-production video shoots. It's in the creative talent to be consistently funny, relevant, and authentic to the TikTok platform.
LinkedIn is generally the most expensive platform, with an average Cost-Per-Click (CPC) often exceeding $5.00.
without: Subscribe to our Sell to Win newsletter!
